generated_from: PoE2DB Waystones and public endgame overview articles checked 2026-07-05; HC notes are curated from access method, boss role, map context, and known endgame mechanics. Video links point to boss-relevant YouTube videos found 2026-07-05 rather than endorsed videos.

bosses.name: The Arbiter of Ash
bosses.access: Burning Monolith after collecting Crisis Fragments from the Iron, Copper, and Stone Citadels.
bosses.summary: Primary endgame pinnacle boss with multi-phase arena pressure and lethal telegraphed attacks.
threats: Pinnacle scaling and arena-wide patterns punish missed movement.
Crisis Fragments are expensive enough that failed learning pulls hurt progression.
Damage windows can bait greedy uptime during one-shot telegraphs.
tips: Do not make the first HC attempt blind; watch patterns and practice the movement plan first.
Enter with capped and overcapped elemental defenses, ailment answers, and a setup built for bossing rather than mapping.
Prioritize living through each phase over forcing damage; leave the arena if focus or flasks are compromised.

bosses.name: The Arbiter of Divinity
bosses.summary: Major storyline boss tied to the current endgame tower route.
threats: Endgame storyline boss pressure with limited public HC familiarity.
Tower progression can encourage rushing into the fight after several maps.
Unknown or evolving mechanics are especially dangerous for HC attempts.
tips: Scout the fight on softcore/video first and treat the first HC pull as a survival rehearsal.
Do not enter after exhausting flasks or attention on the tower path; reset before the boss.
Avoid experimenting with new gear, passives, or keybinds on the character that opens the attempt.

bosses.name: Count Geonor
bosses.summary: Citadel version of the Act 1 boss; part of the Crisis Fragment path toward the Arbiter of Ash.
threats: Cold and corruption-themed phase pressure can punish poor movement.
Citadel attempts are progression-critical and should not be treated as ordinary maps.
Wolf phase mobility can catch builds with weak recovery or slow reactions.
tips: Practice the campaign Geonor pattern and overprepare cold mitigation before the citadel version.
Enter with a bossing setup, not a clear-speed setup, and avoid risky tablet or map pressure.
If adds or ground effects clutter the arena, reset position instead of tunneling damage.

bosses.name: Jamanra, the Abomination
bosses.summary: Citadel version of the Act 2 boss and one of the Crisis Fragment guardians.
threats: Large elemental patterns and phase transitions can cover the arena.
The fight blocks Ritual at the boss area, so the boss itself is the main check.
Underprepared characters can be clipped by familiar campaign mechanics at endgame values.
tips: Bring strong lightning and elemental mitigation and review Jamanra's campaign tells before HC.
Use surrounding Copper Citadel region maps to prepare, not to gamble with extra danger.
Do not chase damage during large arena patterns; move first, attack second.

bosses.name: Doryani, Royal Thaumaturge
bosses.summary: Citadel version of the Act 3 Vaal boss and a Crisis Fragment guardian.
threats: Vaal machinery effects can make telegraphs visually busy.
Beam and area-denial patterns punish standing still or finishing long animations.
Citadel stakes make blind learning especially expensive.
tips: Treat Doryani as a dedicated bossing session with capped elemental defenses and practiced movement.
Lower visual clutter where possible and do not fight through overlapping machinery effects.
Avoid combining the attempt with extra atlas pressure; survival matters more than reward scaling.

bosses.name: Precursor Separator
bosses.summary: Gateway boss from the endgame storyline path.
threats: Gateway bosses are progression checks with less familiar patterns than normal map bosses.
Mechanical or precursor-themed effects can create sudden burst windows.
The fight may appear after a map route that has already spent flasks and focus.
tips: Reset before entering the boss space and keep movement skills for mechanic dodges.
Treat the first clear as a scouting attempt; avoid damage and recovery penalties.
If the boss creates constructs or hazards, kill or reposition around them before committing damage.

bosses.name: Precursor Refiner
bosses.summary: Gateway boss from the endgame storyline path.
threats: Gateway progression can hide boss mechanics behind a normal map rhythm.
Refiner-style hazards may punish standing inside repeated ground effects.
Low public familiarity increases blind-HC risk.
tips: Fight at medium range until the pattern is clear rather than facetanking early tells.
Keep the arena edge known and avoid getting pinned by machinery or ground effects.
Skip risky waystone combinations until you have one clean kill recorded.

bosses.name: Phyx, Sentinel of the Spark
bosses.summary: Sentinel boss associated with the Patriarch Halls quest area.
threats: Spark-themed attacks imply fast elemental projectile pressure.
Quest-area boss attempts can be under-scouted compared with farmed pinnacles.
Arena pressure may punish builds with slow recovery after shocks or hits.
tips: Bring shock and lightning answers before committing a HC character.
Move diagonally through projectile patterns instead of retreating in a straight line.
Keep damage windows short until you know which attacks can overlap.

bosses.name: Phya, Sentinel of the Cradle
bosses.summary: Sentinel boss associated with the Matriarch Halls quest area.
threats: Sentinel mechanics can layer area denial with direct attacks.
Quest-area boss data may be incomplete or change with patches.
Poor positioning can make the arena feel much smaller than it is.
tips: Scout the arena first and keep an exit lane before starting long casts or channels.
Avoid reduced recovery and extra monster damage until the pattern is familiar.
If cradle or construct hazards spawn, reposition first and resume damage after the floor is readable.

bosses.name: The Aberration
bosses.summary: Powerful endgame quest boss tied to the current atlas storyline.
threats: Powerful boss marker indicates a fight above normal map-boss expectations.
Aberration-style attacks may be visually chaotic or corruption-themed.
Experience lock suggests a special encounter rather than a routine farming map.
tips: Treat this as a real boss attempt even if it appears inside an atlas quest chain.
Do not learn it with recovery penalties or multiple damage modifiers active.
Keep enough burst control to handle adds or spawned hazards without losing boss visibility.

bosses.name: The Bodach
bosses.summary: Powerful boss from the Ruins of Kingsmarch endgame route.
threats: Powerful boss marker and no experience change indicate a special encounter.
Kingsmarch ruins can make ground effects and add pressure hard to read.
Unknown burst windows are dangerous for HC on first contact.
tips: Enter with full flasks and a conservative map setup; do not treat it like a regular ruins boss.
Clear any surrounding pressure before engaging so the fight remains readable.
Use short test damage windows until the boss's largest attack is identified.

bosses.name: The Raven Trickster
bosses.summary: Powerful endgame boss with trickster-style pressure.
threats: Trickster-style bosses can punish target fixation and poor arena awareness.
Withered Willow context suggests dangerous ground or debuff pressure.
Special boss marker raises the baseline risk above normal map bosses.
tips: Keep the boss visible and avoid chasing illusions or movement into uncleared space.
Bring debuff and recovery answers before trying the fight on an important HC character.
If the boss disappears or relocates, reset position rather than attacking blind.

bosses.name: Vessel of Kulemak
bosses.summary: Powerful endgame boss tied to soul-themed atlas progression.
threats: Soul-themed mechanics may involve adds, debuffs, or repeated arena pressure.
Powerful boss marker means normal map-boss instincts may understate the danger.
The fight can become risky if recovery is pressured for too long.
tips: Keep add control and single-target damage both available; do not enter with a pure mapper setup.
Reset whenever the floor becomes unreadable instead of trying to finish a damage cycle.
Avoid reduced recovery and extra damage combinations until the fight is understood.

bosses.name: Medved, the Fallen Seer
bosses.summary: One of the powerful expedition island bosses listed in the endgame map set.
threats: Expedition remnants can make the boss far more dangerous than its baseline.
Bog terrain can restrict movement and hide lingering ground effects.
Experience lock and powerful boss markers indicate a serious encounter.
tips: Read remnant modifiers before starting; never give the boss immunities or damage stacks your build cannot handle.
Clear the route and fight from dry, open ground where possible.
If remnants make recovery or resistances unreliable, skip the boss on HC.

bosses.name: Vorana, Last to Fall
bosses.summary: Powerful expedition boss encounter with HC danger from remnant scaling.
threats: Expedition modifiers can turn manageable attacks into lethal hits.
Cold pasture context can combine movement pressure with elemental danger.
Vorana-style bosses tend to reward patience and punish greed.
tips: Choose remnants for loot only after checking they do not brick your defenses.
Keep space to move before detonating toward the boss marker.
If the boss gains too much damage or speed from remnants, abandon the attempt.

bosses.name: Uhtred, the Stardrinker
bosses.summary: Powerful expedition boss in a secluded temple encounter.
threats: Expedition remnant choices can sharply increase boss damage or disable defenses.
Temple arenas can be visually busy and punish standing in layered effects.
Powerful boss marker makes this a step above normal map bosses.
tips: Do not chain explosives into the boss marker until the surrounding path is clear.
Avoid remnants that reduce your recovery or pierce your primary mitigation.
Fight with enough room to leave star or temple effects before resuming damage.

bosses.name: Olroth, Origin of the Fall
bosses.access: Expedition logbooks and endgame expedition boss routes.
bosses.summary: Expedition pinnacle boss reached through logbook/endgame expedition progression.
threats: Expedition remnants and boss scaling can stack into lethal combinations.
The fight is valuable and tempting to over-invest, which increases HC risk.
Boss patterns punish builds that cannot recover while moving.
tips: Plan the expedition chain around safety first; do not path through remnant mods that counter your build.
Bring a true boss setup with reliable recovery outside of kill chains.
If the logbook route creates bad modifiers, save the character and skip the boss.

bosses.name: The King in the Mists
bosses.access: Ritual progression and Crux of Nothingness.
bosses.summary: Ritual pinnacle boss with dangerous control and arena mechanics.
threats: Ritual-style re-fights and control effects can snowball quickly.
The boss is listed as a powerful map boss in Crux of Nothingness.
Mist and arena effects can reduce visibility and punish panic movement.
tips: Bring answers to slows, control, and recovery pressure before attempting on HC.
Do not chase the boss through mist or adds; reset to readable space first.
Avoid damage mods while learning the ritual phase order and arena tells.

bosses.name: It That Was Tul
bosses.summary: Breach-themed powerful boss encounter tied to Tul.
threats: Breach encounters combine boss danger with high monster density.
Frozen Lairs imply cold pressure and movement restriction risk.
Powerful boss marker indicates the fight can spike above normal mapping.
tips: Bring freeze/chill answers and do not fight while surrounded by breach adds.
Clear enough space before pushing damage so cold ground and monsters do not box you in.
Avoid extra monster speed until you can comfortably control breach density.

bosses.name: It That Was Esh
bosses.summary: Breach-themed powerful boss encounter tied to Esh.
threats: Breach density can overlap with lightning projectile or rift pressure.
Sparking Rifts imply shock and lightning burst risk.
Boss visibility can suffer when breach monsters flood the arena.
tips: Overprepare lightning and shock mitigation before attempting on HC.
Thin breach monsters before focusing the boss so projectiles stay readable.
Move diagonally across rift pressure instead of backing straight into fresh spawns.

bosses.name: Xesht, We That Are One
bosses.summary: Breach pinnacle boss reached after farming Breach Splinters.
threats: Breach boss pressure rewards high damage but punishes poor movement.
Dense adds and arena mechanics can overwhelm weak recovery.
The encounter costs Breachstone access, tempting players to overforce attempts.
tips: Do not attempt on HC until your build can clear dense breach packs without panic movement.
Bring reliable recovery that does not depend entirely on killing small monsters.
Learn the major telegraphs before scaling breach difficulty for atlas points.

bosses.name: Kosis
bosses.summary: One of the major Simulacrum bosses from Delirium endgame.
threats: Delirium scaling increases monster pressure while boss mechanics are active.
Kosis can punish low chaos/elemental mitigation and poor arena control.
Simulacrum waves can drain flasks before the boss becomes the main threat.
tips: Do not enter late waves without enough flask sustain and ailment coverage.
Prioritize wave control before boss damage; extra monsters make Kosis far more dangerous.
Leave greed at the door on HC; Simulacrum rewards are not worth a weak recovery setup.

bosses.name: Omniphobia
bosses.summary: One of the major Simulacrum bosses from Delirium endgame.
threats: Simulacrum wave density can overlap boss pressure with constant monster attacks.
Fear-themed lunges and burst windows punish standing still.
Delirium fog makes visual clarity worse as difficulty rises.
tips: Keep moving in controlled arcs and avoid getting trapped against Simulacrum edges.
Save burst and defensive cooldowns for boss windows rather than spending them on normal waves.
Stop pushing waves if recovery, visibility, or focus starts to degrade.

bosses.name: The Trialmaster
bosses.summary: Ultimatum boss where prior trial choices can make the final fight much more dangerous.
threats: Ultimatum modifiers stack danger before the boss even starts.
Arena constraints punish builds that need lots of setup room.
Greedy trial choices can remove recovery windows or add lethal overlap.
tips: Choose Ultimatum modifiers as if the boss is guaranteed; do not build a brick wall for yourself.
Skip choices that counter your recovery or movement on HC even if the reward looks good.
Enter the boss with cooldowns ready and enough flask charges to survive a long phase.

bosses.name: Zarokh, the Temporal
bosses.access: Trial of the Sekhemas / Djinn Barya progression.
bosses.summary: Trial boss where time-themed mechanics and trial attrition can make HC attempts risky.
threats: Trial attrition can leave the character weaker before the boss begins.
Time-themed mechanics punish delayed reactions and poor positioning.
Mistakes can be expensive because the run has already consumed time and resources.
tips: Preserve defenses during the trial; do not arrive at Zarokh with your safety layers exhausted.
Learn the time mechanics before attempting on a character you are unwilling to lose.
Favor consistent movement and recovery over maximum damage during the final phase.
